en.extract:
The aberrant bush warbler (Horornis flavolivaceus) is a species in the bush warbler family, Cettiidae. It was formerly included in the "Old World warbler" assemblage.
It is found in central and southern China, and the northern extremes of Myanmar and Vietnam. It occurs in the countries of Bhutan, China, India, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar, Nepal, Philippines, Thailand, Timor-Leste and Vietnam and also may occur in northeastern and southeastern Bangladesh.


Taxonomy

The aberrant bush warbler was formally described in 1845 by the English zoologist Edward Blyth based on a specimen collected in Nepal. He placed the species in the genus Neornis and coined the binomial name Neornis flavolivacea. The specific epithet flavolivaceus combines the Latin flavus meaning "yellow" with the Modern Latin olivaceus meaning "olive-green". The aberrant bush warbler is now placed in the genus Horornis that was introduced in 1845 by Brian Hodgson.
Thirteen subspecies are recognised:

H. f. flavolivaceus (Blyth, 1845) – central, east Himalayas
H. f. stresemanni (Koelz, 1954) – southwest Assam (northeast India)
H. f. weberi (Mayr, 1941) – southeast Assam (northeast India) and west Myanmar
H. f. intricatus (Hartert, EJO, 1909) – north, east Myanmar to south China
H. f. oblitus (Mayr, 1941) – north Indochina
H. f. sepiarius (Kloss, 1931) – montane north Sumatra
H. f. flaviventris (Salvadori, 1879) – montane central, south Sumatra
H. f. vulcanius (Blyth, 1870) – montane Java, Bali, Lombok and Sumbawa (west Lesser Sunda Islands)
H. f. kolichisi (Johnstone, RE & Darnell, 1997) – montane Alor Island (east Lesser Sunda Islands)
H. f. everetti (Hartert, EJO, 1898) – montane Timor and Wetar (east Lesser Sunda Islands)
H. f. banksi (Chasen, 1935) – montane northwest Borneo
H. f. oreophilus (Sharpe, 1888) – montane northeast Borneo
H. f. palawanus (Ripley & Rabor, 1962) – montane Palawan (southwest Philippines)
The last eight subspecies in the above list have sometimes been considered as a separate species, the Sunda bush warbler Horornis vulcanius.

The '''aberrant bush warbler''' ('''''Horornis flavolivaceus''''') is a [[species]] in the bush warbler [[Family (taxonomy)|family]], [[Cettiidae]]. It was formerly included in the "[[Old World warbler]]" assemblage.

It is found in central and southern [[China]], and the northern extremes of [[Myanmar]] and [[Vietnam]].<ref>{{cite book |author=John Ramsay MacKinnon |author2=Karen Phillipps |author3=Fen-qi He |title=A field guide to the birds of China |publisher=Oxford University Press |year=2000 |isbn=978-0-19-854940-6 |url=https://archive.org/details/fieldguidetobird00john |url-access=registration |page=[https://archive.org/details/fieldguidetobird00john/page/10 10] |quote=Aberrant Bush-warbler.}}</ref> It occurs in the countries of [[Bhutan]], [[China]], [[India]], [[Indonesia]], [[Laos]], [[Malaysia]], [[Myanmar]], [[Nepal]], [[Philippines]], [[Thailand]], [[Timor-Leste]] and [[Vietnam]]<ref name="iucn"/> and also may occur in northeastern and southeastern [[Bangladesh]].<ref name="oiseaux">{{cite web |title=Aberrant Bush Warbler - Horornis flavolivaceus |url=http://www.oiseaux.net/birds/aberrant.bush.warbler.html |website=Les oiseaux}}</ref>

==Taxonomy==
[[Image:Aberrant Bush Warbler 0A2A4060.jpg|thumb|left|''H. f. vulcanius'']]
The aberrant bush warbler was [[species description|formally described]] in 1845 by the English zoologist [[Edward Blyth]] based on a specimen collected in Nepal. He placed the species in the genus ''Neornis'' and coined the [[binomial name]] ''Neornis flavolivacea''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last=Blyth | first=Edward | author-link=Edward Blyth | year=1845 | title=Notices and descriptions of various new or little known species of birds (continued) | journal=Journal of the Asiatic Society of Bengal | volume=14, Part 2 | issue=164 | pages=546–602 [590] | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/40127365 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor1-last=Mayr | editor1-first=Ernst | editor1-link=Ernst Mayr | editor2-last=Cottrell | editor2-first=G. William | year=1986 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=11 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=14 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14483715 }}</ref> The specific epithet ''flavolivaceus'' combines the [[Latin]] ''flavus'' meaning "yellow" with the [[Neo-Latin|Modern Latin]] ''olivaceus'' meaning "olive-green".<ref>{{cite book |last=Jobling |first=James A. |year=2010 |title=The Helm Dictionary of Scientific Bird Names |publisher=Christopher Helm |location=London |isbn=978-1-4081-2501-4 |page=162 |url=https://archive.org/stream/Helm_Dictionary_of_Scientific_Bird_Names_by_James_A._Jobling#page/n162/mode/1up}}</ref> The aberrant bush warbler is now placed in the genus ''[[Horornis]]'' that was introduced in 1845 by [[Brian Houghton Hodgson|Brian Hodgson]].<ref name=ioc>{{cite web |editor1-last=Gill |editor1-first=Frank |editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) |editor2-last=Donsker |editor2-first=David |editor3-last=Rasmussen |editor3-first=Pamela |editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen |date=January 2023 |title=Cupwings, crombecs, bush warblers, Streaked Scrub Warbler, yellow flycatchers, hylias |work=IOC World Bird List Version 13.1 |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/crombecs/ |publisher=International Ornithologists' Union |access-date=12 February 2023}}</ref>

Thirteen [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=ioc/>
* ''H. f. flavolivaceus'' ([[Edward Blyth|Blyth]], 1845) – central, east Himalayas
* ''H. f. stresemanni'' ([[Walter Koelz|Koelz]], 1954) – southwest Assam (northeast India)
* ''H. f. weberi'' ([[Carl Eduard Hellmayr|Mayr]], 1941) – southeast Assam (northeast India) and west Myanmar
* ''H. f. intricatus'' ([[Ernst Hartert|Hartert, EJO]], 1909) – north, east Myanmar to south China
* ''H. f. oblitus'' (Mayr, 1941) – north Indochina
* ''H. f. sepiarius'' ([[C. Boden Kloss|Kloss]], 1931) – montane north [[Sumatra]]
* ''H. f. flaviventris'' ([[Tommaso Salvadori|Salvadori]], 1879) – montane central, south [[Sumatra]]
* ''H. f. vulcanius'' (Blyth, 1870) – montane [[Java]], [[Bali]], [[Lombok]] and [[Sumbawa]] (west [[Lesser Sunda Islands]])
* ''H. f. kolichisi'' (Johnstone, RE & Darnell, 1997) – montane [[Alor Island]] (east [[Lesser Sunda Islands]])
* ''H. f. everetti'' (Hartert, EJO, 1898) – montane [[Timor]] and [[Wetar]] (east [[Lesser Sunda Islands]])
* ''H. f. banksi'' ([[Frederick Nutter Chasen|Chasen]], 1935) – montane northwest [[Borneo]]
* ''H. f. oreophilus'' ([[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1888) – montane northeast [[Borneo]]
* ''H. f. palawanus'' ([[Sidney Dillon Ripley|Ripley]] & [[Dioscoro S. Rabor|Rabor]], 1962) – montane [[Palawan]] (southwest Philippines)

The last eight subspecies in the above list have sometimes been considered as a separate species, the Sunda bush warbler ''Horornis vulcanius''.<ref name=ioc/><ref name=bow>{{cite journal |last1=Limparungpatthanakij |first1=W.L. |last2=Clement |first2=P. |last3=Kirwan |first3=G.M. |year=2022 |title=Aberrant Bush Warbler (''Horornis flavolivaceus''), version 2.1 |editor-last=Billerman |editor-first=S.M. |journal=Birds of the World |location=Ithaca, NY, USA |publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology |doi=10.2173/bow.abbwar1.02.1}}</ref>

zh.extract:
异色树莺（学名：Horornis flavolivaceus）为鶲科树莺属的鸟类。该物种的模式产地在尼泊尔。


亚种
异色树莺西南亚种（学名：Horornis flavolivaceus dulcivox）。在中国大陆，分布于四川、云南等地。该物种的模式产地在四川。
异色树莺指名亚种（学名：Horornis flavolivaceus flavolivaceus）。在中国大陆，分布于西藏等地。该物种的模式产地在尼泊尔。
异色树莺秦岭亚种（学名：Horornis flavolivaceus intricatus）。在中国大陆，分布于山西、陕西、四川、山东等地。该物种的模式产地在陕西。
